articles.title: USER GROUPS ARE NOW AVAILABLE IN FIGMA
articles.summary: User groups in Figma enable bulk sharing of files, projects, and teams, with automatic access granted to all current and future group members. Content can be shared directly through the share modal, streamlining permission management across multiple resources. Groups can also be mentioned in comments and FigJam using @Group name to notify all members simultaneously.

articles.title: IBM INVESTS IN GENERATIVE AI APP DESIGN STARTUP ANIMA
articles.summary: IBM invested in generative AI startup Anima, which specializes in "design-to-code" technology that converts UI designs into functional code using AI. Anima's platform integrates with design tools like Figma and Adobe XD to automatically generate frontend code, helping bridge the gap between designers and developers in the "vibe coding" trend. The investment will help Anima expand its enterprise integrations. The company is already working with major clients like Samsung, Amazon, and Apple to accelerate design projects by up to 50%.

articles.title: GALAXY S26 AND S26 PLUS COLORS REVEALED IN CLEAN RENDERS
articles.summary: A new leak from Evan Blass reveals that Samsung's upcoming Galaxy S26 and S26 Plus will launch in four finishes—Black, White, Blue, and Cobalt Violet—with matching frames and a refined but largely unchanged design, keeping both models visually aligned. Rather than a dramatic exterior overhaul, Samsung appears to be focusing on AI-driven software enhancements as the main selling point, meaning buyers will likely choose between the two based on size, battery, and chip differences rather than color or design.

articles.title: USEFUL GUIDELINES AND CALCULATORS FOR UX RESEARCH
articles.summary: UX research should challenge assumptions and reduce risk rather than validate pre-made decisions. Start with small sample sizes (5 interviews per segment) to identify critical issues before scaling up. Effective research requires clear goals first, then appropriate questions, methods, and sample sizes, while triangulating both quantitative behavioral data and qualitative motivational insights.

articles.title: DESIGN IS DEAD, IT'S ALL EVOLUTION NOW
articles.summary: Traditional product design with intentional structure and hierarchy has been replaced by evolutionary development, where teams make hundreds of random changes and keep only the successful ones. Instagram exemplifies this shift, evolving from a simple photo timeline into a chaotic mix of features like Stories, Reels, and IGTV that lack logical organization. This approach eliminates the need for human designers since the process mirrors natural evolution, where variants are generated variants and survivors are selected without conscious planning.

articles.title: THE ART OF THE FLOP: WHY DESIGN NEEDS THE COURAGE TO FAIL
articles.summary: The Museum of Failure debuted in the UK at Packaging Innovations & Empack 2026, showcasing infamous flops like the Tetra Pak Rigello bottle and the noisy Sun Chips compostable bag to highlight how “good” failure—experimentation that pushes boundaries—drives innovation. Founder Samuel West and collaborator Ricardo Campos argue that industries must foster psychological safety, avoid groupthink, and critically manage AI use to prevent costly overconfidence, emphasizing that smart experimentation and learning from mistakes are essential to real progress.

articles.title: ADD AI FEATURES TO YOUR SITE
articles.summary: Scaffold is a free platform that lets users build custom AI tools and chatbots without coding or API keys, then embed them anywhere on their site.

articles.title: EFFORTLESS AI IMAGE AND VIDEO GENERATION
articles.summary: Somake is an AI creative suite that generates stunning images and videos from text descriptions or uploaded images, offering over 300 AI-powered tools, including background removal, image upscaling, and video creation.

articles.title: YOUR THINKING SPACE WITH AI
articles.summary: Dessix.io combines traditional brainstorming and ideation tools with AI assistance. Users can leverage this digital workspace to organize their thoughts and generate ideas more effectively with AI support.

articles.title: HOW AI LITERACY SHAPES GENAI SSE
articles.summary: AI literacy involves two distinct skills: prompt fluency (the ability to communicate effectively with AI) and output literacy (the ability to evaluate and verify AI responses). Research identified four user types based on these skills: AI novices (low in both), naive power users (good at prompting but poor at evaluation), skeptical abstainers (good at evaluation but avoid using AI), and AI experts (strong in both areas). Understanding these different literacy levels is crucial for designing inclusive AI experiences that support users across all skill levels.

articles.title: APPLE CREATOR STUDIO AI USAGE LIMITS SEEM DRAMATICALLY LOWER THAN PROMISED
articles.summary: Developer Steve Troughton-Smith found that creating a single AI-generated Keynote presentation consumed 47% of his monthly Apple Creator Studio limit—far from the “50 presentations per month” Apple suggested—meaning he could realistically make only two, while building an entire app with Xcode's Codex used just 7% of his weekly allowance. The stark contrast, also highlighted by John Gruber, raises questions about how Apple calculates AI usage across tools and whether the advertised limits reflect real-world use.

articles.title: FIGMA VARIABLES STILL DON'T UNDERSTAND SYSTEMS
articles.summary: Figma variables are good at storing values, but fail to show how those values relate to each other or are derived from base values. Most design systems use a few base values that are modified across different states, but Figma variables don't support this composition model. This post proposes a lightweight CMS-like system within Figma that would allow variables to reference shared structures and automatically generate predictable token sets with proper error handling.

articles.title: WHY YOUR BRAIN REBELS AGAINST REDESIGNS — EVEN GOOD ONES
articles.summary: Major redesigns trigger user backlash because they disrupt the brain's ability to perform familiar tasks without conscious thought, forcing users to revert to effortful, deliberate thinking that feels exhausting.

articles.title: ADOBE'S CREATIVE COLLECTIVE ASSEMBLES MULTIDISCIPLINARY EXPERTS TO ANALYSE AND ADVISE ON THE INDUSTRY'S BIG CHANGES
articles.summary: The Adobe Creative Collective is a cross-disciplinary group formed to help navigate rapid changes in the creative landscape, particularly around AI.
